data.title: ETFs with heavy Netflix and Warner Bros. allocations are set to ride the media shakeup
data.description:
Friday’s market spotlight has been on Netflix (NFLX), which announced plans to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD)—including its film and television studios—for an enterprise value of approximately $82.7 billion.

The blockbuster deal has captured widespread attention across the media and communication services sectors, highlighting the ongoing consolidation shaping the entertainment industry. Investors and fund managers are also watching closely, as both companies are widely held across exchange-traded funds.

Current data shows that roughly 432 million Netflix shares are spread across 483 ETFs, while Warner Bros. Discovery accounts for about 594 million shares in 313 funds. The concentration of holdings underscores the potential ripple effects this merger could have across diversified portfolios.

Outlined below are Wall Street’s top 10 ETFs that have the largest portfolio claims in in NFLX and WBD:

Top 10 ETFs with the Largest Exposure to NFLX

No. 1: T-Rex 2X Long NFLX Daily Target ETF (NFLU), 66.35% allocation.

No. 2: Roundhill NFLX WeeklyPay ETF (NFLW), 19.95% allocation. 

No. 3: Direxion Daily NFLX Bull 2X Shares (NEXL), 12.87% allocation. 

No. 4: First Trust Dow Jones Internet Index Fund (FDN), 9.13% allocation. 

No. 5: FT Vest Dow Jones Internet & Target Income ETF (FDND), 8.26% allocation. 

No. 6: YieldMax Dorsey Wright Hybrid 5 Income ETF (FIVY), 8.27% allocation. 

No. 7: Invesco Next Gen Media and Gaming ETF (GGME), 7.28% allocation. 

No. 8: Global X PureCap MSCI Communication Services ETF (GXPC), 6.99% allocation. 

No. 9: Direxion Daily Dow Jones Internet Bull 3X Shares (WEBL), 6.64% allocation.

No. 10: Direxion Daily NYSE FANG+ Bull 2X Shares (FNGG), 5.79% allocation. 

Top 10 ETFs with the Largest Exposure to WBD 

No. 1: Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight Communication Services ETF (RSPC), 9.41% allocation.

No. 2: S-Network Streaming & Gaming Index - Benchmark TR Net (BNGE), 6.69% allocation. 

No. 3: ProShares Nasdaq-100 Dorsey Wright Momentum ETF (QQQA), 6.46% allocation. 

No. 4: Communication Services Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLC), 6.28% allocation. 

No. 5: Invesco Leisure and Entertainment ETF (PEJ), 5.16% allocation. 

No. 6: Adaptiv Select ETF (ADPV), 5.03% allocation.

No. 7: ProShares Ultra Communication Services (LTL), 4.51% allocation. 

No. 8: Pinnacle Focused Opportunities ETF (FCUS), 4.38% allocation. 

No. 9: First Trust Dorsey Wright Momentum & Value ETF (DVLU), 4.05% allocation. No. 10: Fidelity Disruptive Communications ETF (FDCF), 3.88% allocation.

data.title: Atlanta Fed lowers Q3 GDP growth estimate after soft PCE data
data.description:
The Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta's GDPNow model estimate for third-quarter real gross domestic product growth was revised down to a +3.5% seasonally adjusted annual rate from +3.8% on a day earlier, the regional Fed bank said on Friday. That compares with the official +3.8% in Q2.

The pullback follows the latest Personal Consumption Expenditures report published by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis, ultimately signaling a moderate slowdown in consumer spending before the government shutdown started on Oct. 1.

Real personal consumption growth was flat M/M in September, decelerating from the previous month's +0.2%, which was revised from +0.4%, the delayed BEA report showed.

As such, the nowcast for Q3 real PCE growth fell to 2.7% from 3.1%, the Atlanta Fed said.

The next GDPNow update will arrive by Dec. 11, and the first model nowcast for Q4 GDP growth will not occur before Dec. 23.

data.title: OpenAI 'code red' response to Google could come as soon as next week: report
data.description:
The next version of OpenAI's (OPENAI) GPT model, GPT-5.2, could be publicly unveiled as soon as next week, The Verge reported, as it looks to catch up to Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) in the generative artificial intelligence space.

The GPT-5.2 update is expected to close the gap between GPT and Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Gemini 3, the news outlet added, citing sources. The release of GPT-5.2 had been scheduled for the middle of December, but could come as soon as Dec. 9, the news outlet added.

OpenAI did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

OpenAI CEO Sam Altman reportedly issued a “code red” memo to employees earlier this month, telling staff at the ChatGPT maker they need to speed up fixes and improvements for its chatbot amid increased competition from Google. He called for a “surge” effort to improve the chatbot, which would delay work on other projects such as autonomous AI agents and advertising.

Following last month's release of Gemini 3, Alphabet shares have been on a strong upswing, and the family of models have received strong praise from industry analysts and tech executives, including Altman and xAI CEO Elon Musk. Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff said he would not return to ChatGPT after using Gemini 3.

Investment firm Coatue noted that the stocks of companies in the OpenAI ecosystem, including Nvidia (NVDA), AMD (AMD) and others, have seen sharp price corrections since the release of Gemini 3, while those inside the Google ecosystem have benefited. However, Coatue co-founder Philippe Laffont added that he expects a rebound in the stocks of the OpenAI ecosystem.

data.title: STOXX 600 registers weekly gain; Fed decision in focus
data.description:
Update: The post reflects European markets closing indices.

London (UKX) -0.45% to 9,667. 

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.61% to 24,028. Germany’s factory orders rose 1.5% M/M in October.

France (CAC:IND) -0.09% to 8,115. France trade deficit deteriorates in October. Industrial production in France increased 0.2% M/M in October. 

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) ended Friday's session flat at 578.9, securing a second week of gains as investors weighed next year’s rate outlook, the payoff from surging AI capex, and prospects for a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire.

Investors await next week’s Federal Reserve policy decision in focus. The central bank is expected to deliver another 25 basis point rate cut next week, with 2–3 more reductions anticipated next year. 

The GDP in the Euro Area expanded 0.30% in Q3 2025 over the previous quarter, above estimates of 0.2%.

In the bond market, the yield on the US 10-year Treasury was up 2 basis points at 4.13%.

Germany's 10-year yield was unchanged at 2.80%.

UK's 10-year yield was unchanged at 4.48%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

Seeking Alpha Editor Khushi Singh contributed to this article.

data.title: KKR said to invest in portfolio of BAM-backed Compass Datacenters
data.description:
Private equity giant KKR (KKR) is making a new bet on AI infrastructure, agreeing to invest in some of the operating data centers and future assets of Compass Datacenters, which is backed by Brookfield Asset Management (BAM), according to a media report on Friday.

The deal is anticipated to raise several billion dollars, Bloomberg reported, citing a person with knowledge of the matter and an emailed statement from Compass Datacenters, as it seeks to build new data centers.

The move reflects how alternative investment firms like Blackstone (BX) and KKR (KKR) are increasingly moving toward the AI infrastructure boom to support the expanding demands of major tech companies.

In 2023, Brookfield (BAM) and existing stakeholder Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan agreed to buy Compass Datacenters from Redbird Capital Partners and Azrieli Group.

Neither Brookfield (BAM) nor KKR (KKR) immediately responded to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

data.title: Orla Mining plunges as investor sells C$441M stake
data.description:
Orla Mining (ORLA) -9.1% in Friday's trading after investor Fairfax Financial Holdings said it sold C$441.1 million (US$316 million) worth of its shares as part of a portfolio investment rebalancing; the stock has still more than doubled YTD.

Fairfax said it sold 25 million common shares in the Canadian miner at a price of C$17.6435 each.

Following the sale, Fairfax's ownership stake in Orla (ORLA) fell to nearly 31.82 million shares, while it retained the same principal amount of notes and warrants; its holding now represents ~9.4% of the miner's issued and outstanding shares, or 19.8% on a partially diluted basis.

data.title: EU fines Musk's X €120M for breaching bloc's online rules
data.description:
The European Commission said Friday it has fined Elon Musk's X €120M over the social media platform's process to obtain "blue checkmarks," lack of transparency of its ad repository, and restriction of access to public data, which are in breach of the bloc's Digital Services Act. 

According to the Commission, the easy availability of verified status on X makes it difficult for users to judge an account's authenticity and exposes them to scams.

They allege X uses design features and access barriers, such as excessive delays in processing, which undermine the purpose of ad repositories, and the platform also imposes unnecessary barriers on researchers who wish to access public data.

"Deceiving users with blue checkmarks, obscuring information on ads, and shutting out researchers have no place online in the EU. The DSA protects users," said Henna Virkkunen, a senior official at the EC.

FCC Chairman Brendan Carr took it to X in support of Musk's company and called out Europe and its efforts to target U.S. Big Tech. 

"Once again, Europe is fining a successful U.S. tech company for being a successful U.S. tech company," Carr said. "Europe is taxing Americans to subsidize a continent held back by Europe’s own suffocating regulations."

data.title: Nvidia reveals its biggest expansion to CUDA since its 2006 launch
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) has launched CUDA 13.1 and CUDA Tile, which the Jensen Huang-led company said is the most substantial advancement to the platform since its release about 20 years ago.  

"This exciting innovation introduces a virtual instruction set for tile-based parallel programming, focusing on the ability to write algorithms at a higher level and abstract away the details of specialized hardware, such as tensor cores," said Nvidia engineers Jonathan Bentz and Tony Scudiero in a blog post.

CUDA is a parallel computing platform and programming model created by Nvidia. It helps developers increase the speed of their applications by harnessing the computing power of graphics processing units. 

The new tile-based programming option allows developers to have "fine-grained control over" how their code is executed, particularly across multiple GPU architectures.

"Tile-based programming enables you to program your algorithm by specifying chunks of data, or tiles, and then defining the computations performed on those tiles," Bentz and Scudiero noted. "You don't need to set how your algorithm is executed at an element-by-element level: the compiler and runtime will handle that for you."

CUDA Tile is available in Python. Nvidia plans to release a version compatible with C++ at some point in the future.

"It's just come out in Python, and I think that maps beautifully to the AI development framework," said Stephen Jones, Nvidia's CUDA architect. "We are intentionally releasing the CUDA Tile frontier language as an open-source project."

data.title: Dell warns PC customers of price increases amid rising memory costs: report
data.description:
• Dell Technologies (DELL) has warned PC customers that it could raise prices between significantly, amid rising costs for memory, Trendforce reported.
  • The price increases could be between 15% and 20% and could hit as soon as the middle of this month, the news outlet added, citing industry sources.
  • Dell did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • Memory chips, which are widely assumed to account for as much as 15% to 18% of a PC cost, have been in strong demand for months, amid the continued artificial intelligence boom. HP Enterprise (HPE) said this week that its memory costs have risen, though management expressed confidence that it will be able to pass off the price increases to consumers.
  • Micron (MU), one of the largest memory suppliers, said this week that it will end its Crucial consumer business as part of its ongoing portfolio transformation to align its business to “secular, profitable growth vectors in memory and storage.”
  • Separately, Citi upped its price targets on Seagate (STX) and Western Digital (WDC) this week, citing “sustained” momentum in storage.

data.title: Stellantis in default of Canadian aid package for moving Jeep line to US
data.description:
The Canadian government will formally serve Stellantis (STLA) with a default notice in regards to its CAD500M ($358M) aid package as a result of the carmaker’s decision to move production of the Jeep Compass SUV to the U.S. from the Brampton, Ontario plant. 

"We’ll stand firm for the sake of our workers, our industries and our nation because defending these jobs means defending Canada’s economic backbone and the livelihoods of countless families,” Canadian Industry Minister Melanie Joly said to the House of Commons' international trade committee.

Stellantis' (STLA) move from Brampton to Belvidere is part of the carmakers' plan to invest $13B over the next four years in U.S. manufacturing facilities to mitigate the impact from U.S. trade policy.

In October, Joly threatened legal action against Stellantis (STLA) if the company continued with plans to move Jeep Compass production out of the country.  Joly considered a move to Illinois in violation of Stellantis’ agreement to “maintain its full Canadian footprint, including Brampton.”

“We were there for the company in 2009 to pull it back from the brink of bankruptcy, and now we expect you to be there for Canadians,” Joly wrote in a letter to Stellantis CEO Antonio Filosa, referencing the Canadian government’s contribution to the 2009 bailout of General Motors and Chrysler.

But with import tariffs threatening Stellantis’ (STLA) already fragile bottom-line, the company pledged a substantial investment to move production into the U.S. to help offset ~$1.74B in tariff costs.

Of the $13B, Stellantis (STLA) said it will allocate $600M to reopen the Belvidere, Illinois Assembly Plant to expand Jeep output. The plant, idled in February 2023 amid cost-savings measures, had been a major sticking point for the autoworker’s union, which secured assurances of its reopening during 2023 contract negotiations.

The potential legal dispute with Canada has done little to dent Stellantis’ (STLA) upward momentum with shares higher for a fourth consecutive day with a cumulative gain of 15%.
